etymology.description: phonetic 是(是) (shì) + semantic 頁(頁) (yè, "head") -> "forehead"
etymology.interpretation: The traditional character 題 combines the phonetic element 是(是) (shì) for its sound with the semantic element 頁(頁) (yè, "head") to denote the 'forehead'. The notion of 'forehead' relates to the front or main part of the head, leading to the meaning 'title' or 'subject,' as these are forefront considerations.
etymology.simplification: Simplified from 題 by replacing 頁(頁) (yè, "head") with 页(頁), reducing stroke count while retaining the sound element 是(是) (shì).

examples.chinese: 这次考试的题很难(這次考試的題很難)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè cì kǎoshì de tí hěn nán.
examples.english: The exam topics this time are difficult.

examples.chinese: 老师让我们准备五个题目(老師讓我們準備五個題目)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī ràng wǒmen zhǔnbèi wǔ gè tímù.
examples.english: The teacher asked us to prepare five topics.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢写与科学相关的题材(他喜歡寫與科學相關的題材)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān xiě yǔ kēxué xiāngguān de tícái.
examples.english: He likes to write about topics related to science.
